description: This map displays satellite imagery of an unexploited peat bog near Benderloch, Wester Scotland, United Kingdom, with land cover classification polygons overlaid. The central feature is a large, irregularly shaped area of peat bog, characterized by a distinct reddish-pink hue in the satellite imagery, contrasting with surrounding green and brown vegetated areas and dark blue water bodies to the right. This central peat bog area is delineated by a polygon outlined in yellow. An accompanying text label within the image identifies this area as "CORINE Land Cover (CLC), Peat bog (412)" and "MAES (Mapping and Assessment of Ecosystems and their Services): Unexploited peat bogs (7212)". Other adjacent land cover features are also outlined with yellow polygons. The delineation of the peat bog is sourced from a Nature 2000 habitat map. The satellite imagery credit is ESA.
